claim: Longitudinal MC evidence maps become safer and more scientifically useful when observations are temporally aligned by absolute timestamp, observation window, lag uncertainty, and source boundary before any cross-record pattern is inferred.
source_status: synthetic fixture for research organization; no clinical, veterinary, or personal source used
claim_status: testable infrastructure claim, not medical or veterinary claim

missingness.rule: Missing fields remain unknown; missingness must not be interpreted as absence or improvement.

revision_reason: Adds temporal-alignment validation to the forge so longitudinal evidence maps do not collapse sequence, lag, co-occurrence, and causality.
testability: Executable validator checks temporal anchors, observation windows, lag fields, measurable variables, safe routes, blocked promotions, privacy state, and falsification route.
falsification_route: The hypothesis fails if records without absolute time anchors, observation windows, lag uncertainty, or source boundaries are allowed to generate pattern claims.

measurable_variables.name: absolute_timestamp_present
measurable_variables.measurement_context: Each record must include an ISO 8601 timestamp or explicit unknown state.

measurable_variables.name: observation_window_minutes
measurable_variables.measurement_context: Estimated duration over which the observation was collected.

measurable_variables.name: lag_uncertainty_minutes
measurable_variables.measurement_context: Uncertainty between candidate trigger/context and observed change. Null is allowed only when temporal relation is unknown.

measurable_variables.name: temporal_relation_class
measurable_variables.measurement_context: before, after, during, same_window, unknown, or not_applicable.

next_executable_action: Run python tools/hypothesis_test_forge/validate_temporal_alignment_fixture.py and inspect rejected records for unsafe temporal inference.

records.claim: A subjective state-shift can be logged as a temporal observation without asserting diagnosis, treatment response, urgency, or causality.
records.missingness: Missing: objective biomarker, clinician interpretation, exact preceding exposures, and independent replication.
records.revision_reason: Separates sequence from causality and preserves lag uncertainty.
records.testability: Passes only if timestamp, observation window, lag uncertainty, and relation class are explicit.
records.falsification_route: Fails if same-window co-occurrence is promoted as cause, diagnosis, treatment instruction, or certainty.

temporal_anchor.candidate_context: context switch during reflective AI session

source_boundaries: Self-report style input is not equivalent to medical examination.
Temporal proximity is not causal evidence.
AI-generated structuring is not independent confirmation.

measurable_variables.name: reported_transition_intensity
measurable_variables.measurement_context: Subjective rating logged at the end of the observation window.

measurable_variables.name: task_context_label
measurable_variables.measurement_context: Context label assigned before downstream inference.

measurable_variables.name: lag_uncertainty_minutes
measurable_variables.measurement_context: Estimated uncertainty between context and reported transition.

records.next_executable_action: Store as a deidentified temporal record and compare only with records using compatible windows.

records.claim: Animal-care observations can be prepared for professional review by preserving timing, window, and missingness without giving veterinary advice.
records.claim_status: vet question-prep infrastructure, not veterinary advice
records.missingness: Missing: veterinarian exam, diagnostic tests, professional plan, dose history, environmental controls, and objective trend baseline.
records.revision_reason: Prevents an observed change after a context event from being treated as proof of cause or improvement.
records.testability: Passes only if professional-review framing, timing uncertainty, and blocked veterinary advice classes are present.
records.falsification_route: Fails if the record instructs medication, asserts diagnosis, or treats missing follow-up as normal.
source_boundaries: Owner-style observation is not a veterinary diagnosis.
Post-event timing is not proof of mechanism.
Question-prep output must not include dosage or treatment instructions.

measurable_variables.name: appetite_observation_state
measurable_variables.measurement_context: Owner-style observation prepared for veterinarian discussion.

measurable_variables.name: activity_change_count
measurable_variables.measurement_context: Number of observed behavior changes within the window.

measurable_variables.name: lag_uncertainty_minutes
measurable_variables.measurement_context: Uncertainty from candidate context to observed change.

records.next_executable_action: Export as a timing-aware question-prep row for professional review, not as advice.

records.claim: A health-related note can be temporally indexed for later review while blocking diagnosis, treatment, urgency, and certainty claims.
records.missingness: Missing: clinician assessment, formal test results, baseline measurement protocol, confounders, and validated outcome measure.
records.revision_reason: Adds temporal comparability before cross-session pattern inference.
records.testability: Passes only if comparable timing fields and missingness labels are present.
records.falsification_route: Fails if the record converts temporal alignment into diagnosis, treatment, or proof.
source_boundaries: Personal observation is not a clinical finding.
Candidate context is a research variable, not a confirmed trigger.
Temporal alignment is infrastructure for questions, not triage.

measurable_variables.name: ambient_condition_label
measurable_variables.measurement_context: Context label for later comparison, not a confirmed cause.

measurable_variables.name: observation_presence_flag
measurable_variables.measurement_context: Logged observation state with unknown allowed.

records.next_executable_action: Keep as a deidentified longitudinal row and require professional-review framing before export.

records.claim: AI-assisted pattern detection should mark temporal gaps and uncertainty before proposing a new inference.
records.missingness: Missing: independent observer, raw transcript, exact environmental timeline, and external validation.
records.revision_reason: Prevents AI from smoothing over time gaps when building coherent narratives.
records.testability: Passes if unknown temporal relation remains unknown and blocks narrative certainty.
records.falsification_route: Fails if the AI assigns sequence or causality when the timestamp or relation class is unknown.
source_boundaries: Memory-like continuity is not a timestamped record.
Coherent narrative is not evidence of sequence.
Unknown timing must block causal or trend claims.

measurable_variables.name: temporal_relation_class
measurable_variables.measurement_context: Unknown must remain unknown unless evidence supports assignment.

measurable_variables.name: narrative_certainty_score
measurable_variables.measurement_context: Confidence cap for unverified temporal reconstruction.

records.next_executable_action: Route to contradiction ledger until timestamp or source evidence is recovered.

negative_controls.unsafe_text: Because the observation happened after the context, this proves the context caused it.
No follow-up data means the issue was absent.
This timing record is enough to choose a care action without professional review.
